Outlook

data.title: AGNC Investment swings to a loss in Q1 on Middle East conflict uncertainty
data.description:
Overview

  • U.S. mortgage REIT reported Q1 net loss and tangible net book value per share fell 5.6% from $8.88 per common share as of December 31, 2025

  • Company posted Q1 comprehensive loss per share and negative 1.6% economic return on tangible equity

  • Net spread and dollar roll income per share rose from prior quarter; $401 mln equity issued via ATM program

Outlook

  • Company says longer-term outlook for Agency MBS remains constructive despite near-term geopolitical risks

  • AGNC sees wider mortgage spreads to benchmark rates and improved supply-demand as positive catalysts for Agency MBS

  • Company says interest rate volatility and Middle East tensions add uncertainty to near-term outlook

Result Drivers

  • SPREAD WIDENING - Agency MBS spreads widened in March due to increased volatility and negative investor sentiment following the war in Iran, leading to a decline in tangible net book value and negative economic return

  • HIGHER NET INTEREST SPREAD - Net spread and dollar roll income per share increased from the prior quarter, driven by a 25 basis point rise in net interest spread

  • CAPITAL MANAGEMENT - Over $400 mln of common equity was issued through the at-the-market program, supporting capital strategy and liquidity

Company press release:

data.title: Arch Capital Group Increases Buyback Authorization by $3B
data.description:
By Elias Schisgall

Arch Capital Group increased its stock buyback authorization by $3 billion.

The insurance and reinsurance company said it has $3.1 billion available for buybacks as of Monday, after taking into account the increased authorization and recent repurchases of shares.

Write to Elias Schisgall at elias.schisgall@wsj.com

data.title: Wall Street closes slightly down on renewed tensions between US, Iran
data.description:
Key points:
  • QXO falls after $17 bln TopBuild acquisition deal
  • Meta drops more than 2% after nine straight sessions of gains
  • Earnings to pick up pace this week
  • Indexes off: Dow 0.01%, S&P 500 0.24%, Nasdaq 0.26%

By Chuck Mikolajczak and Purvi Agarwal

U.S. stocks closed slightly lower on Monday, with each of the three major indexes coming off a third straight week of gains, as renewed U.S.-Iran tensions put the durability of a two-week ceasefire in question.

Iran is considering attending peace talks with the U.S. in Pakistan, a senior Iranian official told Reuters, following moves by Islamabad to end a U.S. blockade of Iran's ports.

However, a separate source said Vice President JD Vance was still in the U.S., denying reports he was on his way to Pakistan for talks.

Iran opened the Strait of Hormuz on Friday, fueling a broad market surge, with the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq posting record highs for a third straight session for their biggest weekly gains in 11 months. However, Tehran closed the crucial shipping waterway again over the weekend.

U.S. crude jumped 6.87% to settle at $89.61 a barrel and Brent rose to settle at $95.48 per barrel, up 5.64% on the day, lifting the S&P 500 energy index 0.21%.

"The news over the weekend with the re-closure of the strait or the boarding of the Iran vessel, that gets us a little away from it's fully reopened, but the timing doesn't look like it's still that far off as it was with at least talks over the week," said Tom Hainlin, national investment strategist at U.S. Bank Wealth Management in Minneapolis.

"But you're in the middle of first-quarter earnings season too, so then the question is, has there been any bleed over into the real economy, and so far you've heard from the banks that consumer credit looks okay and their spending looks okay."

The Dow Jones Industrial Average shed 4.87 points, or 0.01%, to 49,442.56, the S&P 500 lost 16.92 points, or 0.24%, to 7,109.14 and the Nasdaq Composite declined 64.09 points, or 0.26%, to 24,404.39.

Communication services was the worst-performing sector, as Meta was down 2.56% to snap a nine-session winning streak, its longest since October.

Netflix fell 2.55% to also weigh on the sector, and has fallen about 12% since announcing its quarterly results and the departure of co-founder Reed Hastings last week.

The CBOE Volatility Index , known as Wall Street's "fear gauge", gained after falling for the past eight sessions and was last up 1.37 points at 18.85, after reaching a one-week high of 19.99.

Investors will look to assess the impact of the Iran war on corporate earnings and on the broader economy, with companies including Lockheed Martin and IBM scheduled to report later this week.

Tesla will kick off results from the so-called "Magnificent Seven" group of megacap stocks on Wednesday.

Of the 48 S&P 500 companies that have reported earnings through Friday morning, 87.5% have topped analyst expectations, according to LSEG data. The current first-quarter earnings growth rate stands at 14.4%.

Among other movers, QXO shares lost 3.12% after the construction supplies distributor struck a $17 billion deal to acquire building products distributor and installer TopBuild , whose shares surged 19.38%.

Advancing issues outnumbered decliners by a 1.08-to-1 ratio on the NYSE, but declining issues outnumbered advancers by a 1.01-to-1 ratio on the Nasdaq.

The S&P 500 posted 44 new 52-week highs and no new lows while the Nasdaq Composite recorded 173 new highs and 42 new lows.

Volume on U.S. exchanges was 16.42 billion shares, compared with the 18.54 billion average for the full session over the last 20 trading days.

data.title: Wheat gains on cold snap and drought; corn up as rain to delay planting
data.description:
Key points:
  • USDA crop report expected to show slight decline in winter wheat condition
  • Cold snap adds to drought stress on US winter wheat crop
  • Rain may delay Midwest corn and soybean planting, forecasters warn

By Karl Plume

U.S. wheat futures jumped on Monday as a weekend cold snap in parts of the Plains and Midwest fuelled worries about damage to the winter crop that has already been stressed by drought.

Gains, however, were kept in check by forecasts for rain in the driest areas of the winter wheat belt.

Corn followed wheat futures higher as cold temperatures and a rainy outlook threatened Midwest planting. Soybeans traded on both sides of unchanged but ended down slightly.

All three commodities were supported by sharply higher crude oil prices amid fears that the ceasefire between the United States, Israel and Iran may break down.

Grain traders looked ahead to the U.S. Department of Agriculture's crop progress and conditions report due later on Monday. Analysts polled by Reuters, on average, expect the USDA to rate 33% of winter wheat in good-to-excellent shape as of Sunday, down one point from a week earlier.

"The crop conditions report this afternoon is probably going to depict continued drought, but I feel like the market is holding back on taking prices much higher because there's rain in the forecast," said Mike Zuzolo, president of Global Commodity Analytics.

Chicago Board of Trade May wheat (WK26) settled up 5-3/4 cents at $5.97 per bushel. CBOT May corn (CK26) gained 3-1/4 cents to $4.52 a bushel while May soybeans (SK26) were up 1/2 cent at $11.67-3/4 a bushel.

Rain was likely to disrupt corn and soybean planting in parts of the Midwest over the next two weeks, forecasters said.

Analysts expect the USDA to report that 11% of the U.S. corn crop and 12% of soybeans have been planted as of Sunday.

data.title: CNB Financial's Q1 net income rises on ESSA acquisition boost
data.description:
Overview

  • U.S. regional bank's Q1 net income and EPS rose yr/yr, driven by ESSA Bancorp acquisition

  • Organic loan balances declined for Q1 due to prepayments in large CRE loans

Outlook

Result Drivers

  • TREASURY MANAGEMENT - Deposit growth was driven by expanded Treasury Management activity among municipal deposit relationships, as well as growth in corporate and wholesale deposits

  • LOWER NON-INTEREST EXPENSE - Adjusted earnings rose slightly from the prior quarter, mainly due to lower non-interest expense, including reduced salaries, benefits, and state/local taxes

data.title: Zions reports 1Q26 net earnings $232M, diluted EPS $1.56; CET1 11.5%
data.description:
Zions reported first-quarter 2026 net earnings of $232 million, or diluted EPS of $1.56, versus $169 million and $1.13 in 1Q25. Pre-provision net revenue (PPNR) was $298 million (adjusted PPNR $301 million) and taxable-equivalent net interest income was $673 million; the bank estimated its common equity tier 1 ratio at 11.5% and tangible book value per common share rose to $41.75. Net loan and lease charge-offs remained minimal at $4 million (0.03% annualized) while total deposits grew to $76.9 billion.

Financial Highlights

  • Net earnings: $232 million for 1Q26 (net earnings applicable to common shareholders).
  • Diluted earnings per common share: $1.56 for 1Q26.
  • Net interest income: $662 million; taxable-equivalent net interest income: $673 million; net interest margin: 3.27%.
  • Pre-provision net revenue (PPNR): $298 million; adjusted PPNR: $301 million.
  • Capital and book values: estimated CET1 ratio 11.5%; tangible book value per common share $41.75.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix improved as lower funding costs and growth in higher-yielding loans increased net interest income 6% year-over-year.
  • Customer-related noninterest income rose 9% year-over-year to $172 million, led by higher loan-related fees and retail/business banking fees.
  • Loans and leases increased 2% year-over-year to $61.3 billion, driven by growth in commercial loans (+$860 million) and consumer loans (+$458 million, including home equity lines).
  • Funding profile strengthened: total deposits up 2% to $76.9 billion with customer deposits (ex-brokered) up 3%; brokered deposits down 20% and short-term borrowings down 89% vs. prior year.
  • Credit quality remained strong — annualized net loan charge-offs 0.03%; nonperforming assets $292 million (0.48% of loans and OREO); classified loans declined 19% year-over-year.

Original SEC Filing: ZIONS BANCORPORATION, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION /UT/ [ ZION ] - 8-K - Apr. 20, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: MSP Recovery Secures Two $0.1 Million One-Time Advances From Hazel and VRM to Bolster Liquidity
data.description:
MSP Recovery disclosed two small liquidity actions on April 16, 2026: a $0.1 million discretionary advance from Hazel Partners Holdings under its existing working capital credit facility and a separate $0.1 million one-time advance of recovery proceeds from VRM MSP Recovery Partners. The Hazel advance was funded at Hazel’s sole discretion and does not restore or expand ongoing borrowing capacity. The VRM advance supports accounts payables and must be reimbursed upon any new financing, including potential debtor-in-possession financing. The company emphasized that neither agreement provides committed or recurring liquidity.

Agreement 1: MSP Recovery Receives $0.1 Million Discretionary Advance From Hazel for Operating Needs

  • Agreement type: Discretionary one-time advance under working capital credit facility
  • Counterparty: Hazel Partners Holdings
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 16 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: One-time; at will
  • Reason: Provide limited operating expense liquidity

Agreement 2: MSP Recovery Obtains $0.1 Million One-Time Recovery Proceeds Advance From VRM

  • Agreement type: One-time advance of recovery proceeds
  • Counterparty: VRM MSP Recovery Partners
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 16 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: One-time; at will
  • Reason: Support near-term accounts payable

Original SEC Filing: MSP Recovery, Inc. [ MSPR ] - 8-K - Apr. 20, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Faraday Future Raises $45 Million Via Notes Purchase, Adding Equity-Linked Redemption Features
data.description:
Faraday Future secured $45 million through a Note Purchase Agreement with an accredited investor, issuing a $15.78 million A-1 Note at 9% and a $30.0 million secured B Note at 3.5%, both maturing in 24 months. The structure includes stock or cash redemption options tied to Nasdaq Minimum Price, a share reserve, and the ability to exchange portions of the B Note into A Notes under certain conditions. The B Note is backed by a Deposit Account Control Agreement with Lakeside Bank, a Pledge Agreement, and a Guaranty by certain subsidiaries. Univest Securities acted as placement agent, earning an 8% cash fee and a six-month tail on introduced financings. The company expects the package to bolster liquidity and preserve flexibility.

Agreement 1: Faraday Future Raises $45 Million Via Notes Purchase, Adding Equity-Linked Redemption Features

  • Agreement type: $45 million note purchase agreement with equity-linked redemptions
  • Counterparty: Accredited Investor
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 17 2026 / Apr 17 2026
  • Duration / Termination: 24 months
  • Reason: Enhance liquidity and financial flexibility

Agreement 2: Faraday Future Issues $15.78 Million A-1 Note at 9% With Share Redemption Option

  • Agreement type: A-1 Promissory Note issuance
  • Counterparty: Accredited Investor
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 17 2026 / Apr 17 2026
  • Duration / Termination: 24 months
  • Reason: Provide working capital and structured liquidity

Agreement 3: Faraday Future Issues $30 Million Secured B Note at 3.5% Backed by Deposit Account

  • Agreement type: Secured B Promissory Note issuance
  • Counterparty: Accredited Investor
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 17 2026 / Apr 17 2026
  • Duration / Termination: 24 months
  • Reason: Access lower-cost secured capital

Agreement 4: Faraday Future Executes Deposit Account Control Agreement to Secure B Note

  • Agreement type: Deposit Account Control Agreement
  • Counterparty: Lakeside Bank and Accredited Investor
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 17 2026 / Apr 17 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Until B Note repaid
  • Reason: Provide collateral control to secure obligations

Agreement 5: Faraday Future Enters Pledge Agreement to Support Secured Financing

  • Agreement type: Pledge Agreement
  • Counterparty: Accredited Investor
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 17 2026 / Apr 17 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Until obligations repaid
  • Reason: Enhance collateral coverage for secured note

Agreement 6: Faraday Future Subsidiaries Provide Guaranty Backing Secured Note Obligations

  • Agreement type: Guaranty Agreement
  • Counterparty: Accredited Investor
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 17 2026 / Apr 17 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Until obligations repaid
  • Reason: Strengthen credit support for financing

Agreement 7: Faraday Future Engages Univest Securities as Placement Agent for Note Financing

  • Agreement type: Placement Agency Agreement
  • Counterparty: Univest Securities
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 17 2026 / Apr 17 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Transaction-based; 6-month tail
  • Reason: Facilitate and place financing with investors

Original SEC Filing: FARADAY FUTURE INTELLIGENT ELECTRIC INC. [ FFAI ] - 8-K - Apr. 20, 2026

data.title: FMC Amends Syndicated Credit Agreement, Adds Subsidiary Guarantees and Collateral; Sets 3.5x Secured Leverage Cap
data.description:
FMC entered into Amendment No. 6 to its Fifth Amended and Restated Credit Agreement to modify financial covenants and enhance lender protections. The amendment adjusts the maximum leverage and minimum interest coverage ratios for certain quarters and introduces a maximum secured leverage ratio of 3.50x. FMC designated certain subsidiaries as guarantors and granted security interests and equity pledges to secure obligations. The amendment also tightens negative covenants, including on liens, fundamental changes, indebtedness, and transfers of material assets.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Amendment to existing syndicated credit agreement
  • Counterparty: Citibank, as Administrative Agent, and other lenders
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 16 2026 / same
  • Reason: Adjust covenants and add guaranties and collateral to support financing needs

Original SEC Filing: FMC CORP [ FMC ] - 8-K - Apr. 20, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: CSB Bancorp reports Q1 net income $4.44M, diluted EPS $1.69; ROE 14.03%
data.description:
CSB Bancorp reported first quarter 2026 net income of $4,444,000, or $1.69 per basic and diluted share, up from $3,616,000 and $1.37 a year earlier. Annualized return on average common equity was 14.03% and return on average assets was 1.42% for the quarter. Pre-Provision Net Revenue (PPNR) totaled $6.03 million, a 23% increase versus the prior-year quarter.

Financial Highlights

  • Net income: $4,444,000 for Q1 2026 (Q1 2025: $3,616,000).
  • Diluted (and basic) earnings per share: $1.69 for Q1 2026 (Q1 2025: $1.37).
  • Pre-Provision Net Revenue (PPNR, non-GAAP): $6,032,000 for Q1 2026 (Q1 2025: $4,896,000).
  • Net interest income (GAAP): $11,465,000 for Q1 2026; Net interest income, FTE (non-GAAP): $11,493,000.
  • Return on average common equity (annualized): 14.03%; Return on average assets (annualized): 1.42%.

Business Highlights

  • Average loans increased by $89 million (12% year-over-year), driven by commercial lending including construction draws and term loans for equipment.
  • Average residential mortgage balances rose $16 million (9% year-over-year), with borrower preference for adjustable-rate mortgages retained on the bank’s books.
  • Deposits experienced seasonal declines at quarter end but average deposit balances increased $58 million (6% year-over-year); shift included increases in interest-bearing demand and noninterest-bearing accounts.
  • Commercial construction activity remained robust in the bank’s markets while businesses moderated discretionary borrowing and inventory purchases amid macro uncertainty.
  • Credit quality metrics improved: nonperforming loans declined to $1.0 million (0.12% of loans) and delinquent loans decreased to 0.16% of total loans; net loan charge-offs were minimal at $7,000 for the quarter.

Original SEC Filing: CSB Bancorp, Inc. [ CSBB ] - 8-K - Apr. 20, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Asana directors Matt Cohler, Adam D’Angelo and Lorrie Norrington to resign; Krista Anderson-Copperman named Lead Independent Director
data.description:
Asana announced that directors Matt Cohler, Adam D’Angelo and Lorrie Norrington will resign effective at the 2026 Annual Meeting, and that Krista Anderson-Copperman will succeed Norrington as Lead Independent Director.

IndividualMatt CohlerAdam D’AngeloLorrie NorringtonKrista Anderson-Copperman
RoleDirector (Class I)Director (Class III)Director (Class II); Lead Independent DirectorDirector; Lead Independent Director (designate); Compensation Committee Chair; Audit Committee member
Type of ChangeResigned (effective at 2026 Annual Meeting)Resigned / Not standing for re-election (effective at 2026 Annual Meeting)Resigned (effective at 2026 Annual Meeting)Appointed (to succeed Norrington as Lead Independent Director)
Effective DateAt conclusion of 2026 Annual Meeting (June 8, 2026)At conclusion of 2026 Annual Meeting (June 8, 2026); will remain until thenAt conclusion of 2026 Annual Meeting (June 8, 2026)Effective at conclusion of 2026 Annual Meeting (June 8, 2026)
ReasonPart of orderly Board refreshmentPart of orderly Board refreshment; not standing for re-electionPart of orderly Board refreshmentBoard appointment to succeed departing Lead Independent Director
Replacement InfoNot disclosedNot disclosedKrista Anderson-Copperman to succeed as Lead Independent DirectorN/A
Background DetailsServed on Board since November 2009Served on Board since December 2008Served on Board since July 2019; Lead Independent Director since August 2021Served on Board since July 2022; chairs Compensation Committee; member of Audit Committee
Board/Committee Role ChangesNot disclosedNot disclosedWill be succeeded as Lead Independent DirectorAppointed Lead Independent Director effective at Annual Meeting

data.title: Arch increases share repurchase authorization by $3.0 billion, $3.1B remaining available
data.description:
Arch increased its share repurchase authorization by $3.0 billion, leaving about $3.1 billion available under the program.

Key Highlights:

  • Board raised existing buyback authorization by $3.0 billion on April 19, 2026, to be executed in open market or private transactions.
  • After recent repurchases, approximately $3.1 billion remains available under the program as of April 20, 2026.
  • Timing and size of repurchases will depend on market conditions and corporate and regulatory considerations.

Original SEC Filing: ARCH CAPITAL GROUP LTD. [ ACGL ] - 8-K - Apr. 20, 2026
